facilities.name: Chapman Field Trash and Recycling Center
facilities.address: 13600 Old Cutler Rd, Coral Gables, FL 33158
facilities.hours: Miami-Dade lists neighborhood trash and recycling centers as open daily from 7:00 AM to 5:30 PM. Confirm holiday closures before visiting.
facilities.fees: Neighborhood TRCs are county resident services for eligible residential waste-fee customers, not general public landfills. Oversize vehicles must use a county landfill and pay the per-ton fee.
accepted_materials: Construction and demolition debris up to 3 cubic yards
facilities.rules: Present a valid Florida driver's license or ID that matches an eligible Miami-Dade waste-fee account. Chapman Field does not accept tires, white goods, or mattresses.

accepted_materials: Construction and demolition debris up to 3 cubic yards
facilities.rules: Present a valid Florida driver's license or ID that matches an eligible Miami-Dade waste-fee account. Eureka Drive is one of the centers that accepts used electronics and cardboard recycling.

facilities.name: West Dade Home Chemical Collection Center
facilities.hours: Miami-Dade lists the home chemical collection centers as open Wednesday through Sunday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Confirm holiday schedules before visiting.
facilities.fees: Home chemical drop-off is a Miami-Dade resident service for household quantities. Commercial waste is not accepted.
not_accepted: Materials outside the county home chemical program
facilities.rules: Open to all Miami-Dade County residents. Use the county guidance to confirm packaging requirements before you drive.

facilities.name: South Dade Home Chemical Collection Center
facilities.hours: Miami-Dade lists the home chemical collection centers as open Wednesday through Sunday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Confirm holiday schedules before visiting.
facilities.fees: Home chemical drop-off is a Miami-Dade resident service for household quantities. Commercial waste is not accepted.
not_accepted: Materials outside the county home chemical program
facilities.rules: Open to all Miami-Dade County residents. South Dade uses Gate B. Use the county guidance to confirm packaging requirements before you drive.
